steps.title: Use Prospero's inference
steps.content: Prospero tells Miranda that if she can remember anything from before she came to the island, she may also be able to remember how she came there. He links one recovered pre-island memory to the possibility of another.

steps.title: Reject invented claims
steps.content: A adds melancholy and dissatisfaction, B adds reluctance and clouded judgment, and D says the memory was only a daydream. Prospero instead treats her memory as a basis for recalling her arrival. The answer is C.
